---
title: "Into the programing"
author: "Tio"
date: "2026-05-20"
output:
  html_document: default
  pdf_document: default
---
##setup chunck
```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## Introduction
```{r}

```

```{r workshop-1}
getwd() #my working directory
#------------------------------------#
# MB5370: Introduction to Programming
# Agustinus Satrio Supoyo
# May 2026
# Workshop 1 - Introduction to R
# This script contains basic R programming concepts:
# - Calculations
# - Variables and Assignment
# - Functions
# - Data Types
# - Data Structures
# - Data Frames and Tibbles
# - Packages
#------------------------------------#


#====================================#
# 1. Basic Calculations
#====================================#

2 + 1
1:30
6 * 2


#====================================#
# 2. Variables and Assignment
#====================================#

age <- 25
first_name <- "Bill"

age + 1
age + age

# R is case-sensitive
Age <- 41   # Different from 'age'

# Variables with spaces using backticks
`age bob` <- 25


#====================================#
# 3. Exercise 1 - Basic Calculations
#====================================#

15 + 25.1 + 20.25

total <- 15 + 25.1 + 20.25
Number2 <- 10

total + Number2

final_result <- total + Number2

second_number <- 15 + 23 + 22
total + second_number


#====================================#
# 4. Rounding Functions
#====================================#

years_old <- 25.7

round(years_old)   # Round to nearest whole number
floor(years_old)   # Round down

# Specify decimal places
years_old <- 25.765
round(years_old, 2)

# Help and arguments
?round
args(round)


#====================================#
# 5. Using paste() to Create Sentences
#====================================#

name <- "Agustinus"
age <- 27.5

sentence <- paste(name, "is", age, "years old.")
print(sentence)


#====================================#
# 6. Understanding Variable Updates
#====================================#

grade <- 55
total <- grade + 10
print(total)

grade <- 90
print(total)       # Still uses old value

total <- grade + 10
print(total)       # Updated value


#====================================#
# 7. Simple Multiplication Example
#====================================#

p <- 2
z <- 5

out <- p * z
print(out)


#====================================#
# 8. Data Type Testing
#====================================#

x <- 1

is.character(x)
is.numeric(x)


#====================================#
# 9. Debugging Example
#====================================#

my_quiz <- c(
  "uno",
  "dos",
  "tres",
  "cuatro",
  "cinco"
)

print(my_quiz)
str(my_quiz)
length(my_quiz)


#====================================#
# 10. Data Types
#====================================#

# Character (text)
my_character_variable <- "Agustinus"
class(my_character_variable)

# Numeric (decimal numbers)
my_numeric_variable <- 4.2
class(my_numeric_variable)

# Integer
my_integer_variable <- 10L
class(my_integer_variable)

# Logical (TRUE/FALSE)
my_logical_variable <- TRUE
class(my_logical_variable)


#====================================#
# 11. Data Structures
#====================================#

# List
list("uno", "dos", "tres")

# Numeric vector
y <- c(1, 2, 3)

# Character vector
z <- c("Sarah", "Tracy", "Jon")

# Check class
class(y)
class(z)

# Mixed-type list
x <- list(1, "a", TRUE)

# Access elements
x[[1]]
x[[2]]
x[[3]]


#====================================#
# 12. Data Frames
#====================================#

my_dataframe <- data.frame(
  no = c(1, 2, 3),
  name = c("Tracey", "John", "Pete"),
  passed = c(TRUE, FALSE, TRUE)
)

# View data frame
my_dataframe

# Inspect structure
str(my_dataframe)

# Convert column type
my_dataframe$no <- as.factor(my_dataframe$no)

# Check updated structure
str(my_dataframe)


#====================================#
# 13. Missing Data (NA)
#====================================#

height <- c(170, NA, 180)

# Check missing values
is.na(height)

# Remove missing values
na.omit(height)


#====================================#
# 14. Importing Data
#====================================#

# Read CSV files
#read.csv()

# Read Excel files
#readxl::read_excel()


#====================================#
# 15. Tibbles
#====================================#

library(tibble)

my_tibble <- tibble(
  no = c(1, 2, 3),
  name = c("Tracy", "John", "Pete"),
  passed = c(TRUE, FALSE, TRUE)
)

# View tibble
my_tibble


#====================================#
# 16. Installing and Loading Packages
#====================================#

# Install packages (run only once)
#install.packages("tidyverse")

# Load packages (run every session)
#library(tidyverse)

# Install multiple packages
# install.packages(c("ggplot2", "tidyr", "dplyr", "terra"))

# Open help for a function
?ggplot


#====================================#
# 17. Recommended Script Structure
#====================================#

# 1. Import data
# 2. Clean data
# 3. Analyse data
# 4. Plot results
# 5. Save outputs


#====================================#
# 18. Example Workflow for Data Analysis
#====================================#

# 1. Read the .csv file into R
# 2. Inspect the data structure
# 3. Filter the target species
# 4. Summarise abundance
# 5. Create the plot
# 6. Save the figure

```
##Load Packages##
```{r packages}
library(tidyverse)
```
##ggplot
#| Geom               | Purpose      |
#| `geom_point()`     | Scatter plot |
#| `geom_line()`      | Line chart   |
#| `geom_bar()`       | Bar chart    |
#| `geom_boxplot()`   | Boxplot      |
#| `geom_histogram()` | Histogram    |
#| `geom_smooth()`    | Trend line   |

```{r}
mpg
ggplot(data = mpg) + 
  geom_point(mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy))
```

#Aesthetic mappings
```{r}
 aes() #used to connect variable to visual element
#Element visual example
#color 
#size  
#shape 
#alpha
#linetype 
#group
```

```{r}
#to change the color based on class category
ggplot(data = mpg) +
  geom_point(mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy, colour = class))
```

```{r}
#give the fix color
ggplot(data=mpg)+
  geom_point(mapping=aes(x=displ,y=hwy),colour="blue")
```

```{r}
#Change point size by class
ggplot(data = mpg) +
  geom_point(mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy, size = class))
```

```{r}
##Change point shape by class.
ggplot(data = mpg) + 
  geom_point(mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy, shape = class))
```

```{r}
#transparancy (alpha)
ggplot(data = mpg) +
  geom_point(mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy, alpha = class))
```
```{r}
#
ggplot(data = mpg) +
  geom_point( mapping = aes( x = displ,y = hwy,colour = displ < 5))
```

#Troubleshooting
```{r}
ggplot(data = mpg)+ 
 geom_point(mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy)) 
# the + should be on top line
```

```{r}
ggplot(data = mpg)+
 geom_point(mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy)) 
```

#Facet (Panel Plot)
```{r}
#face wrap
ggplot(data = mpg) +
  geom_point(aes(x = displ, y = hwy)) +
  facet_wrap(~ class, nrow = 2)
```
```{r}
#Face grid
ggplot(data = mpg) +
  geom_point(aes(x = displ, y = hwy)) +
  facet_grid(drv ~ cyl)
```
```{r}
#to add smooth line
ggplot(data=mpg)+
  geom_smooth(mapping=aes(x=displ,y=hwy))
```

```{r}
#to add the line and point together
ggplot(data = mpg) + 
  geom_point(mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy)) + # points horrible 
  geom_smooth(mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy)) # try smooth line
```
```{r}
#to change the line type
ggplot(data = mpg) + 
  geom_smooth(mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy, linetype = drv))
```
```{r}
#Grouping Data with group
ggplot(data = mpg) +
  geom_smooth(mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy, group = drv))
```

```{r}
#Changing Line Color by Group
ggplot(data = mpg) +
  geom_smooth(
    mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy, colour = drv),
    show.legend = FALSE
  )
```

```{r}
#Combining Multiple Geoms
ggplot(data = mpg) +
  geom_point(mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy)) +
  geom_smooth(mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy))
```
```{r}
#Using Global Mappings
ggplot(data = mpg, mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth() #to avoid repeating the same aes()

```

```{r}
#Layer-Specific Mappings
 ggplot(data = mpg) +
  geom_smooth(mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy, group = drv))

ggplot(data = mpg) +
  geom_smooth(
    mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy, colour = drv),
    show.legend = FALSE,
  )

ggplot(data = mpg) + 
  geom_point(mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy)) +
  geom_smooth(mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy))

ggplot(data = mpg, mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy)) + 
  geom_point() + 
  geom_smooth()

ggplot(data = mpg, mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy)) + 
  geom_point(mapping = aes(colour = class)) + 
  geom_smooth()

ggplot(data = mpg, mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy)) + 
  geom_point(mapping = aes(colour = class)) + 
  geom_smooth(data = filter(mpg, class == "subcompact"), se = FALSE)


#Code 1 — Global Mapping
ggplot(data = mpg, mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy)) + 
  geom_point() + 
  geom_smooth()
#Code 2 — Local Mapping
ggplot() + 
  geom_point(data = mpg, mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy)) + 
  geom_smooth(data = mpg, mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy))



```

##Plotting Statistics
```{r}
#create the bar chart
ggplot(data = diamonds) +
  geom_bar(aes(x = cut))
```

```{r}
ggplot(data = diamonds) +
  stat_count(aes(x = cut)) #the same way
```
##Overriding defaults in ggplot2
```{r}
demo <- tribble(
  ~cut,         ~freq,
  "Fair",       1610,
  "Good",       4906,
  "Very Good",  12082,
  "Premium",    13791,
  "Ideal",      21551
)
demo

ggplot(data = demo) +
  geom_bar(mapping = aes(x = cut, y = freq), stat = "identity")
```

```{r}
#override a default mapping from transformed variables to aesthetics
ggplot(data = diamonds) + 
  geom_bar(mapping = aes(x = cut, y = stat(prop), group = 1))
#> Warning: `stat(prop)` was deprecated in ggplot2 3.4.0.


```
##Plotting statistical details
```{r}
#You can do this using stat_summary().
ggplot(data = diamonds) + 
  stat_summary(
    mapping = aes(x = cut, y = depth),
    fun.min = min,
    fun.max = max,
    fun = median
  )

```
```{r}
#aethetic adjustment fill and color
ggplot(data = diamonds) + 
  geom_bar(mapping = aes(x = cut, colour = cut))

ggplot(data = diamonds) + 
  geom_bar(mapping = aes(x = cut, fill = cut))

#to add the clarity
ggplot(data = diamonds) + 
  geom_bar(mapping = aes(x = cut, fill = clarity))


#To alter transparency (alpha)
ggplot(data = diamonds, mapping = aes(x = cut, fill = clarity)) + 
  geom_bar(alpha = 1/5, position = "identity")

#To color the bar outlines with no fill color
ggplot(data = diamonds, mapping = aes(x = cut, colour = clarity)) + 
  geom_bar(fill = NA, position = "identity")
#position = "fill" works like stacking, but scales each bar, so that all bars have the same total height (100%).
ggplot(data = diamonds) + 
  geom_bar(mapping = aes(x = cut, fill = clarity), position = "fill")

#position = "dodge" places overlapping objects directly beside one another.
ggplot(data = diamonds) + 
  geom_bar(mapping = aes(x = cut, fill = clarity), position = "dodge")

#position = "jitter" adds a small amount of random noise to each point to avoid overplotting when points overlap. This is useful for scatterplots but not barplots.
ggplot(data = mpg) + 
  geom_point(mapping = aes(x = displ, y = hwy), position = "jitter")





```
